item.title: What travelers should know about Europe's new Entry/Exit System
item.description: Travel experts say to be prepared for potential disruptions as countries implement the new Entry/Exit System now in place across the EU and other countries.

item.title: Blue Origin vows to resume New Glenn launches despite explosion
item.description: Damage to Blue Origin's lone launch pad in the wake of last week's spectacular explosion was not as severe as initially feared, the company said.

item.title: Trump names controversial housing official Bill Pulte as acting intel chief
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday announced he's tapping housing official Bill Pulte to serve as the acting director of national intelligence to replace Tulsi Gabbard.

item.title: Russian missile, drone attack on Ukraine kills at least 22, officials say
item.description: Russian missile, drone strikes kill at least 22 people across Ukraine, authorities say, after President Zelenskyy warned Moscow was planning a "massive new strike."

item.title: How much interest can you earn on $40,000 now? Here are 4 options to consider.
item.description: There are multiple ways to earn a big return on your $40,000 now (and one specific one that savers should avoid).

item.title: Best private student loans for fall 2026
item.description: Need a private student loan? These lenders offer smart perks, flexible terms and standout borrower benefits.

item.title: Senior Iranian officer says return to war with U.S. seems "inevitable"
item.description: Prospects for a U.S.-Iran deal seem to dim as the war between Israel and Hezbollah grinds on despite Trump saying they agreed to stop fighting.

item.title: Author Marley Dias on importance of telling Black stories: "We all come from somewhere else"
item.description: Marley Dias, an author, activist and recent Harvard graduate, sits down with CBS News 24/7 to discuss her new book, "I Am the Dream Come True."

item.title: Watch: Dr. Oz takes questions on Ebola outbreak, Trump's health at White House press briefing
item.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, administrator for the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, took questions Tuesday's White House press briefing on President Trump's health, the ongoing Ebola outbreak in Africa and more. Following his remarks, CBS News' Nancy Cordes provided additional context.

item.title: Northlake police warn of gas pump screw scam
item.description: The Northlake Police Department issued a warning about a new scam at gas stations that is low tech, but effective.

item.title: Watch: Senator grills Rubio about funds for Trump's "Board of Peace"
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sen. Jacky Rosen pressed State Secretary Marco Rubio about money going into at least two bank accounts to fund President Trump's "Board of Peace" initiative in the Middle East.

item.title: YouTubers have big box office weekend with "Backrooms" and "Obsession"
item.description: Two indie horror films made by YouTubers, "Backrooms" and "Obsession," beat out Disney's latest "Star Wars" movie in the box office this weekend. Steven Zeitchik, senior editor for the Hollywood Reporter, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: 4 things student loan borrowers should do before July 1
item.description: The student loan rulebook is being rewritten, and making a few quick moves now could spare you a costly surprise.

item.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner rescheduled for July 24
item.description: Prosecutors have accused Cole Allen of attempting to assassinate the president when he allegedly attacked the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 25.

item.title: Should you lock in a mortgage interest rate before the June Fed meeting?
item.description: A mortgage interest rate lock before the June Federal Reserve meeting starts may make sense for borrowers. Here's why.

item.title: Watch: Rubio says the Iran war is "over now" as Booker slams proposal to ease sanctions
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio claimed the Iran war "is over now" during his testimony before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee. During a heated exchange with New Jersey Democratic Sen. Cory Booker, Rubio spoke about plans for sanctions against Tehran and denied claims that the Trump administration is seeking a deal similar to the one brokered during the Obama era.

item.title: Bus driver in Virginia crash that killed 5 facing more charges
item.description: Jing Sheng Dong, a 48-year-old tour bus driver from Staten Island, New York, faces three additional felony counts in connection with the deaths.

item.title: Watch: Van Hollen slams "illegal war" in Iran and calls Trump foreign policy a "dumpster fire"
item.description: Maryland Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen slammed State Secretary Marco Rubio during a Senate Foreign Relations Committee hearing over the Trump administration's war in Iran and denounced changes to foreign aid policies and other matters overseas.

item.title: Takeaways from Rubio testimony before Senate Foreign Relations Committee
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio testified before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee Tuesday, giving an update on the war in Iran. CBS News congressional correspondent Taurean Small has more.

item.title: 3 signs that it's time to open a CD account now
item.description: Is it really worth opening a CD account now? Here are three signs indicating that it may be.

item.title: Video shows massive drug-smuggling tunnel connecting U.S. and Mexico
item.description: Four people were arrested and charged with trafficking more than $45 million in cocaine through the 2,000-foot-long tunnel complete with reinforced walls, ventilation and a rail system.

item.title: Trump signs AI executive order to give government early look at new models
item.description: The order asks AI companies to share previews of powerful new models with the government before they are released to the public.

item.title: Watch: Rubio says there's indication that Iran Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is alive
item.description: State Secretary Marco Rubio testified before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee on Tuesday and said that there are indications that Iran's Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is alive, expanding on the communication methods being used to negotiate with Tehran.

item.title: What are Freedom 250 and America250? Behind the 2 groups planning celebrations
item.description: A musical concert series has become a point of political contention, with performers dropping out of the series.

item.title: 6 states hold primaries today, including key races in California, Iowa
item.description: Six states are holding primary contests on Tuesday, including California and Iowa.

item.title: American soldier and British soldier die in training accident in Iraq
item.description: The U.S. and British militaries say one American soldier and one British soldier died during a training exercise in Iraq.

item.title: Vanilla Ice says he'd perform for anybody, including Putin and Iran
item.description: "It's not anything to do with politics. I don't know why they're turning it into politics," Vanilla Ice said of the Freedom 250 concerts planned in Washington, D.C.

item.title: 6/1: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump says Iran talks are continuing, Iran says otherwise; Graham Platner's wife addresses sexting scandal.

item.title: Divided appeals court rules transgender military ban is unconstitutional
item.description: A divided federal appeals court ruled that the Trump administration's policy banning transgender individuals from serving in the military is likely unconstitutional.

item.title: NASA taps 2 companies to develop buggies for its moon base program
item.description: The FLEX Rover will be equipped to carry two astronauts and traverse hundreds of miles of lunar terrain.
